title: Local distortions as a source of piezoelectric/stiffness decoupling in B-doped AlScN
authors: Laszlo Wolf, Geoff L. Brennecka, Vladan Stevanović
abstract: We present a first-principles analysis of the wurtzite pseudo-ternary (Al,Sc,B)N to elucidate the structural origin of a decoupling between stiffness $C_{33}$ and piezoelectric response $e_{33}$ upon boron incorporation, using DFT-relaxed 100-atom special quasirandom structures across a broad composition range. Pair distribution function analysis reveals interstitial threefold-coordinated boron atoms that have displaced from the tetrahedral cation site. Direct structural analysis establishes their preferential orientation along the $c$-axis and identifies a scandium-activated creation mechanism. The vertical coordination asymmetry of each cation is quantified through a site-specific axial asymmetry ratio (AAR), showing that boron incorporation progressively symmetrizes the Sc environment. Correlation with Born effective charges demonstrates that this symmetrization is the mechanism behind the piezoelectric enhancement.
summary: This theoretical study investigates how boron doping affects the mechanical and piezoelectric properties of AlScN. The authors find that the piezoelectric response is enhanced by the symmetrization of the Sc environment, while the stiffness is maintained by local structural changes involving boron. They establish a clear decoupling between these two properties, attributing it to distinct structural mechanisms governing each response.
main_problem: The study aims to elucidate the structural origin of the decoupling between the stiffness component ($C_{33}$) and the piezoelectric response ($e_{33}$) when boron (B) is incorporated into the wurtzite pseudo-ternary (Al,Sc,B)N.
main_result: The decoupling is attributed to the local structural effect of boron atoms moving to a threefold-coordinated, planar configuration, while the piezoelectric enhancement is linked to the progressive symmetrization of the Sc environment, quantified by the Axial Asymmetry Ratio (AAR).
method: The research employs first-principles calculations using DFT (PBE functional) on 100-atom special quasirandom structures, analyzing local geometry via pair distribution functions and quantifying asymmetry using the site-specific Axial Asymmetry Ratio (AAR).
model_or_system: The physical system is the wurtzite pseudo-ternary (Al,Sc,B)N alloy. The analysis focuses on how B incorporation modifies the local coordination environment of Al and Sc cations.
key_observables: Stiffness component $C_{33}$, piezoelectric stress tensor component $e_{33}$ (or $d_{33}$), Axial Asymmetry Ratio (AAR), Born effective charges ($Z^*_{33}$), and local bond lengths.
important_parameters: Boron concentration (B content), lattice parameters ($c/a$ ratio), and the degree of cation environment asymmetry.
main_assumptions: The study uses 100-atom SQSs, and the authors note that the observed decoupling intensity might differ from previous work due to methodological differences.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the evolution of $C_{33}$ and $e_{33}$ with B content, pair distribution functions showing B structural changes, and scatter plots correlating AAR with $Z^*_{33}$ and strain sensitivity for individual atoms (Al, Sc, N).
paper_structure: The paper systematically analyzes the structural changes induced by B doping, correlates these changes (measured by AAR) with electronic properties ($Z^*_{33}$), and then separates the mechanisms governing stiffness ($C_{33}$) versus piezoelectricity ($e_{33}$) to explain the observed decoupling.
why_interesting: While the core physics is solid-state materials science, the rigorous quantification of local structural descriptors (AAR) and their correlation with macroscopic tensor components ($e_{33}$) provides a transferable methodology for understanding symmetry breaking and emergent properties in complex crystal structures.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: Evolution of properties with varying cation substitution, varying colors relate to the fixed content of boron within those series. Left panel is the 33 component in the Voigt notation of the stiffness tensor, middle panel is the 33 component of the piezoelectric stress tensor and right panel presents lattice parameters of the wurtzite alongside their ratio in the inset

Figure 2: Upper panel presents the low-r portion of the total pair distribution function of the different series, vertical shift is applied with increased Sc content. Lower panel is schematic representation of the transition from tetrahedral to planar configuration of a B atom.

Figure 3: Left panel presents definition of the distances involved in the definition of the axial asymmetry ratio (AAR). Right panels are the averaged AAR over Sc and Al atoms for each composition. Error bars represent the standard deviation of the corresponding AAR data set.

Figure 4: Upper panel shows correlation between the AAR and both the Z∗ 33 and the duk dη33 of each individual scandium (triangle) and aluminum (square) atoms of the whole 8%B series. Red-edged marker are cations that present a planar B in their second shell. Lower panel presents the evolution of Z∗ 33 and duk dη33 of all series over the alloying range, where each data point is obtained by averaging over all adequate atoms in the structures.

Figure 5: Evolution of the piezoelectric strain response tensor component d33 with respect to composi- tion for all four series. Colored cross markers are experimental data from AlScN samples[3, 1, 28, 19, 22]
